title: orectanthe sceptrum
side_effects: Orectanthe sceptrum can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and digestive issues. Ingesting its leaves or flowers can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while prolonged exposure to its sap may lead to skin redness and blistering. Overusing the plant in traditional medicine can also cause liver damage, headaches, and fatigue in some individuals. Furthermore, improper handling and preparation of the plant may result in respiratory problems and eye irritation in people sensitive to its volatile compounds.
